mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically increasing present equity values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between FEDFUNDS and 20-day SPX returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating household income prospects that reduce expected consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and payroll employment indicate a joint contraction in demand and labor income that amplifies recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and PAYEMS yields a t-statistic insignificant at the 10 percent level in predicting 60-day returns, or the signal produces positive average returns when triggered.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 230, a federal funds rate above 0.25 percent indicates restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are non-negative in at least 55 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 230 and FEDFUNDS is above 0.25 during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and operating leverage that boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO changes exhibit a Spearman correlation below 0.05 with 60-day SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1200 thousand units signal weakness in residential investment and construction employment that spills over into broad demand for durable goods and building materials, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of instances when HOUST is below 1200 during out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly measures aggregate domestic income and spending power; higher GDP growth raises expected corporate sales growth and equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly growth rate shows a Rank IC below 0.04 with 60-day SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 5.0 percent constitutes a restrictive monetary stance that sharply elevates corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are non-negative in at least 55 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 percent during walk-forward testing.
